input.name: us_007_metric_snapshots
input.query:
-- US-007: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger.
--
-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and the trigger
-- definitions in §15 (lines 1205-1291).
--
-- Three pieces:
--   1. public.metric_snapshots — historized rows, one per Apify run.
--   2. app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE
--      trigger ensuring scrape_mode matches the linked social_link.platform.
--   3. app_private.denorm_metrics — BEFORE INSERT trigger that, for fresh
--      rows, demotes the previous latest under a (social_link, scrape_mode)
--      advisory lock and mirrors the owned columns into creator_profiles.
--
-- Spec gap (story text vs. spec DDL): the story acceptance criterion says the
-- advisory lock is keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode); the spec keys it on
-- (social_link_id, scrape_mode). The two are functionally equivalent because
-- social_links has a partial unique index on (user_id, platform). Following
-- the spec verbatim.

-- =========================================================
-- 1. metric_snapshots table
-- =========================================================
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
  apify_run_id      text,
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,
  following_count   integer,
  total_likes       bigint,
  video_count       integer,
  avg_views_last_10 integer,
  is_verified       boolean,
  raw_payload       jsonb,
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default false,
  error_message     text
);

create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);

-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode); IG has at most two.
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;

-- Idempotency for webhook redeliveries — same Apify run never inserts twice.
create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;

-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans here. Partial keeps it sub-ms.
create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';

-- =========================================================
-- 2. Coherence trigger: scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform
-- =========================================================
create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
declare p platform;
begin
  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
  if p is null then
    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
  end if;
  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_metric_snapshots_coherence
  before insert or update of scrape_mode, social_link_id on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence();

-- =========================================================
-- 3. Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization trigger
-- =========================================================
-- BEFORE INSERT so we can mutate new.is_latest; advisory lock serializes
-- concurrent webhook deliveries / manual refreshes for the same
-- (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
  lock_key bigint;
begin
  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
    new.is_latest := false;
    return new;
  end if;

  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);

  update public.metric_snapshots
     set is_latest = false
   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
     and is_latest;

  new.is_latest := true;

  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at                  = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;
  end if;

  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
  before insert on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();

input.query:
-- Test the trigger: seed a creator + a tiktok social_link, insert two snapshots, verify denorm.
-- Wrap in a transaction and rollback so this leaves no test data behind.
begin;

-- Stand up a creator user + profile + tiktok handle.
with new_user as (
  insert into public.users (role, username, email)
  values ('creator', 'us007_test', 'us007@example.com')
  returning id
), new_profile as (
  insert into public.creator_profiles (user_id, display_name)
  select id, 'US-007 Test' from new_user
  returning user_id
), new_link as (
  insert into public.social_links (user_id, platform, handle, status)
  select user_id, 'tiktok', 'us007creator', 'active' from new_profile
  returning id, user_id
)
select id as social_link_id, user_id from new_link \gset

-- First snapshot: fresh, fetched 1h ago.
insert into public.metric_snapshots (
  social_link_id, scrape_mode, status,
  follower_count, avg_views_last_10, total_likes, video_count, is_verified,
  fetched_at, apify_run_id
) values (
  :'social_link_id', 'tiktok_profile', 'fresh',
  10000, 1500, 200000, 42, false,
  now() - interval '1 hour', 'run_aaa'
);

-- Second snapshot: fresh, fetched now (should win is_latest, demote first).
insert into public.metric_snapshots (
  social_link_id, scrape_mode, status,
  follower_count, avg_views_last_10, total_likes, video_count, is_verified,
  fetched_at, apify_run_id
) values (
  :'social_link_id', 'tiktok_profile', 'fresh',
  12500, 1800, 240000, 44, true,
  now(), 'run_bbb'
);

-- Inspect: only run_bbb is_latest=true; creator_profiles reflects 12500/1800.
select
  (select count(*) from public.metric_snapshots where social_link_id = :'social_link_id') as total_snapshots,
  (select count(*) from public.metric_snapshots where social_link_id = :'social_link_id' and is_latest) as latest_count,
  (select apify_run_id from public.metric_snapshots where social_link_id = :'social_link_id' and is_latest) as latest_run,
  (select tiktok_follower_count from public.creator_profiles where user_id = :'user_id') as denorm_followers,
  (select tiktok_avg_views_last_10 from public.creator_profiles where user_id = :'user_id') as denorm_avg_views,
  (select tiktok_is_verified from public.creator_profiles where user_id = :'user_id') as denorm_verified;

rollback;

content.text: Two new WARN findings on `function_search_path_mutable` for the new functions. Need to pin `search_path = ''` to harden — the SECURITY DEFINER `denorm_metrics` is the riskier one. Adding a follow-up migration.

input.content:
-- US-007 follow-up: pin search_path on the two new trigger functions.
--
-- get_advisors raised `function_search_path_mutable` (WARN) on
-- app_private.denorm_metrics and app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence.
-- denorm_metrics is SECURITY DEFINER, so a caller-controlled search_path could
-- shadow public.creator_profiles or public.metric_snapshots and divert writes.
-- Both functions already qualify every reference, so an empty search_path is
-- safe and clears the advisor.

alter function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
  set search_path = '';

alter function app_private.denorm_metrics()
  set search_path = '';

content.text: `greatest()` is a SQL parser built-in, not a callable function — it can't be schema-qualified. Fixing.

input.old_string:
-- US-007 follow-up #2: requalify trigger function bodies for empty search_path.
--
-- The previous migration set `search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to
-- clear the `function_search_path_mutable` advisor. With an empty search_path,
-- unqualified enum types (e.g. `platform`, `scrape_mode`) and built-ins
-- (`now()`, `greatest()`, `hashtextextended()`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock()`) no
-- longer resolve. We rewrite both function bodies with `public.<type>` casts on
-- enum literals and `pg_catalog.<fn>` on built-ins, and restate
-- `set search_path = ''` because CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION does not preserve
-- prior SET clauses.
input.new_string:
-- US-007 follow-up #2: requalify trigger function bodies for empty search_path.
--
-- The previous migration set `search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to
-- clear the `function_search_path_mutable` advisor. With an empty search_path,
-- unqualified user-defined types (e.g. `platform`, `scrape_mode`) no longer
-- resolve. We rewrite both function bodies with `public.<type>` casts on enum
-- literals and `pg_catalog.<fn>` on regular built-ins. SQL parser built-ins
-- (`GREATEST`, `LEAST`, `COALESCE`) are NOT callable functions and cannot be
-- schema-qualified — they are left bare. `now()` is in pg_catalog and is left
-- bare too (pg_catalog is always implicitly on the search_path even when the
-- user-set portion is empty). Restating `set search_path = ''` because CREATE
-- OR REPLACE FUNCTION does not preserve prior SET clauses.

content.text: I edited a migration file that was already applied — drift risk. Let me revert the file to what was applied and add a new fix migration instead.

content.content:
1	-- US-007 follow-up #2: requalify trigger function bodies for empty search_path.
2	--
3	-- The previous migration set `search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to
4	-- clear the `function_search_path_mutable` advisor. With an empty search_path,
5	-- unqualified user-defined types (e.g. `platform`, `scrape_mode`) no longer
6	-- resolve. We rewrite both function bodies with `public.<type>` casts on enum
7	-- literals and `pg_catalog.<fn>` on regular built-ins. SQL parser built-ins
8	-- (`GREATEST`, `LEAST`, `COALESCE`) are NOT callable functions and cannot be
9	-- schema-qualified — they are left bare. `now()` is in pg_catalog and is left
10	-- bare too (pg_catalog is always implicitly on the search_path even when the
11	-- user-set portion is empty). Restating `set search_path = ''` because CREATE
12	-- OR REPLACE FUNCTION does not preserve prior SET clauses.
13	
14	create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
15	returns trigger
16	language plpgsql
17	set search_path = ''
18	as $$
19	declare p public.platform;
20	begin
21	  select sl.platform into p from public.social_links sl where sl.id = new.social_link_id;
22	  if p is null then
23	    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
24	  end if;
25	  if (p = 'tiktok'::public.platform
26	        and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode)
27	  or (p = 'instagram'::public.platform
28	        and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details'::public.scrape_mode, 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode)) then
29	    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
30	  end if;
31	  return new;
32	end $$;
33	
34	create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
35	returns trigger
36	language plpgsql
37	security definer
38	set search_path = ''
39	as $$
40	declare
41	  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
42	  lock_key bigint;
43	begin
44	  if new.status <> 'fresh'::public.metric_status then
45	    new.is_latest := false;
46	    return new;
47	  end if;
48	
49	  lock_key := pg_catalog.hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
50	  perform pg_catalog.pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);
51	
52	  update public.metric_snapshots
53	     set is_latest = false
54	   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
55	     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
56	     and is_latest;
57	
58	  new.is_latest := true;
59	
60	  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

-- US-007 follow-up #2: requalify trigger function bodies for empty search_path.
--
-- The previous migration set `search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to
-- clear the `function_search_path_mutable` advisor. With an empty search_path,
-- unqualified user-defined types (e.g. `platform`, `scrape_mode`) no longer
-- resolve. We rewrite both function bodies with `public.<type>` casts on enum
-- literals and `pg_catalog.<fn>` on built-ins. Restating `set search_path = ''`
-- because CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION does not preserve prior SET clauses.
--
-- NOTE: this version qualifies `greatest()` and `now()` as `pg_catalog.*`,
-- which is INCORRECT — `GREATEST` is a SQL parser built-in (not a callable
-- function) and cannot be schema-qualified. The runtime error surfaces only
-- when the trigger fires on a fresh tiktok_profile row. Fixed in
-- us_007_fix_function_refs (the next migration).

create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
returns trigger
language plpgsql
set search_path = ''
as $$
declare p public.platform;
begin
  select sl.platform into p from public.social_links sl where sl.id = new.social_link_id;
  if p is null then
    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
  end if;
  if (p = 'tiktok'::public.platform
        and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode)
  or (p = 'instagram'::public.platform
        and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details'::public.scrape_mode, 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode)) then
    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger
language plpgsql
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
declare
  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
  lock_key bigint;
begin
  if new.status <> 'fresh'::public.metric_status then
    new.is_latest := false;
    return new;
  end if;

  lock_key := pg_catalog.hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
  perform pg_catalog.pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);

  update public.metric_snapshots
     set is_latest = false
   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
     and is_latest;

  new.is_latest := true;

  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
           metrics_fetched_at       = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
           metrics_fetched_at       = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           metrics_fetched_at          = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at                  = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;
  end if;

  return new;
end $$;

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user invoked the Marketify Ralph autonomous loop. The agent's task per `scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md` is to:
   - Pick the lowest-priority story with `passes: false` from `scripts/ralph/prd.json`
   - Implement exactly one story per iteration
   - Run quality gates (typecheck, lint, supabase advisors, mobile-mcp for UI stories)
   - Spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before commit
   - Commit with message format `feat: [US-XXX] Story title`
   - Mark `passes: true` in prd.json and append entry to progress.txt
   - If all stories pass, output `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>`
   
   Selected story: **US-007 — Migration: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger** per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and §15 trigger functions (lines 1205-1291).

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Supabase MCP (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) for all DB operations (apply_migration, execute_sql, get_advisors, generate_typescript_types)
   - PostgreSQL trigger functions (BEFORE INSERT) with `pg_advisory_xact_lock` for race-free serialization
   - PostgreSQL `function_search_path_mutable` security advisor and `set search_path = ''` hardening (especially critical for SECURITY DEFINER functions)
   - SQL parser built-ins (`GREATEST`, `LEAST`, `COALESCE`) cannot be schema-qualified
   - `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched even with empty user search_path
   - Append-only migrations stored in `supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDDHHMMSS_us_NNN_description.sql`
   - The denorm trigger branches on `scrape_mode` enum (tiktok_profile, ig_details, ig_posts) and updates only the columns owned by each mode
   - Coherence trigger validates `scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform` mapping
   - Hashed advisory lock key via `pg_catalog.hashtextextended(social_link_id::text || ':' || scrape_mode::text, 0)`
   - Idempotency: `metric_snapshots.apify_run_id` partial unique index prevents webhook redelivery dupes

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **scripts/ralph/prd.json** (read)
     - 65 user stories; identified US-007 as lowest-priority `passes: false`
     - US-001 through US-006 already complete

   - **scripts/ralph/progress.txt** (read)
     - Codebase Patterns section is essential context for every iteration
     - Documented patterns: Supabase project ref `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]`, app_private schema exists, `extensions.citext` qualification needed, `submission_reuse_view` US-009 handoff for RLS interaction

   - **docs/tech-architecture.md** (read offset 562-661, 1180-1300)
     - §4.7 block 6 metric_snapshots DDL
     - §15 trigger functions: `app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()` and `app_private.denorm_metrics()`

   - **supabase/migrations/20260417120000_us_007_metric_snapshots.sql** (CREATED)
     - Created table with 15 columns, 4 indexes (one base, three partial)
     - Two trigger functions per spec §15
     - Spec gap noted: story said advisory lock on (user_id, scrape_mode); spec uses (social_link_id, scrape_mode) — followed spec verbatim
     - Key DDL:
     ```sql
     create table public.metric_snapshots (
       id uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
       social_link_id uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
       scrape_mode scrape_mode not null,
       apify_run_id text,
       status metric_status not null,
       follower_count integer, following_count integer,
       total_likes bigint, video_count integer,
       avg_views_last_10 integer, is_verified boolean,
       raw_payload jsonb,
       fetched_at timestamptz not null default now(),
       is_latest boolean not null default false,
       error_message text
     );
     create unique index metric_snapshots_latest on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;
     create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;
     create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';
     ```

   - **supabase/migrations/20260417120100_us_007_pin_function_search_path.sql** (CREATED)
     - `alter function ... set search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to clear `function_search_path_mutable` WARN

   - **supabase/migrations/20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql** (CREATED, then RESTORED to match what DB actually has)
     - Recreated functions with `public.<type>` casts
     - INTENTIONALLY left the broken `pg_catalog.greatest()` and `pg_catalog.now()` references in the file to match DB state (drift prevention)
     - Header comment notes the bug and points to next migration

   - **supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql** (CREATED — final correct version)
     - Both functions with bare `greatest()` and `now()` (parser built-in / pg_catalog implicit)
     - Both functions with `public.platform`, `public.scrape_mode`, `public.metric_status` casts on enum literals
     - `pg_catalog.hashtextextended()` and `pg_catalog.pg_advisory_xact_lock()` for built-in functions
     - Restated `set search_path = ''` (CREATE OR REPLACE drops prior SET clauses)

   - **src/types/supabase.ts** (REGENERATED via Supabase MCP)
     - Now includes `metric_snapshots` Tables entry with Row/Insert/Update types
     - All 11 enums match spec
     - PostgrestVersion: "14.5"

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **`\gset` syntax error**: Fixed by switching to PL/pgSQL DO block with declared variables
   - **`invalid input value for enum social_link_status: "active"`**: The valid values are `linked | unlinked | failed_fetch`. Fixed by using `'linked'`
   - **`when raise_exception then raise` re-raised the expected coherence error**: Replaced with `when others then v_coherence_caught := true`
   - **RAISE NOTICE messages not visible through MCP**: Captured assertions into a temp table and SELECTed at the end
   - **`type "platform" does not exist`** after pinning empty search_path: Added `public.platform`, `public.scrape_mode`, `public.metric_status` casts on enum literals
   - **`function pg_catalog.greatest(...) does not exist`**: GREATEST is a SQL parser construct, not a callable function. Fixed by using bare `greatest()` (and `now()` similarly bare since pg_catalog is always implicitly searched)
   - **Drift risk from editing already-applied migration file**: Per established codebase pattern ("editing the local `.sql` file afterwards drifts from the migrations table"), I restored the broken file to match DB state and wrote a NEW fix migration

5. Problem Solving:
   - All trigger behaviors verified end-to-end via DO block tests:
     - 2 tiktok snapshots → newest claims is_latest=true, older demoted; denorm columns reflect newest values (followers=12500, avg_views=1800, verified=true)
     - Coherence trigger correctly rejects `ig_details` on a tiktok platform link
     - `refreshing` status rows do NOT claim is_latest and do NOT clobber denorm
     - IG split denorm: both `ig_details` and `ig_posts` rows on same link both stay is_latest=true (count=2) without clobbering each other
     - Apify run_id idempotency: duplicate insert on same `apify_run_id` triggers unique_violation
   - Final advisor state: zero WARN/ERROR; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (expected, deferred to US-009)

6. All user messages:
   - **System message** (initial): Ralph loop instructions + "Pick lowest priority unfinished story" workflow
   - **User message after ToolSearch loaded Supabase MCP tools**: "Tool loaded." (with system-reminder about TodoWrite)
   - **User message after ToolSearch loaded TodoWrite**: "Tool loaded."
   - **User message (final)**: Request to write a detailed conversation summary in <analysis> + <summary> blocks, with explicit instructions NOT to call any tools

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Run typecheck + lint quality gates (`npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx expo lint`)
   - Spawn `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with diff scope, US-007 acceptance criteria, doc pointers
   - Stage specific files (not `git add -A`) and commit with `feat: [US-007] Migration: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger`
   - Update prd.json: set `passes: true` for US-007
   - Append dated entry to progress.txt with the full Ralph format including:
     - What was implemented
     - Files changed
     - Quality checks line
     - Code-reviewer verdict
     - Learnings for future iterations
   - Promote any general/reusable learnings to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at top of progress.txt
   - Specifically promote the `set search_path = ''` + qualification pattern (and the GREATEST gotcha) since this will recur on every future trigger function migration

8. Current Work:
   Immediately before this summary request, I had just finished writing `src/types/supabase.ts` with the regenerated types that include the new `metric_snapshots` table. The TodoWrite list state was:
   - 1-6: completed (verify enums, write migration, apply, test trigger, advisors, regenerate types)
   - 7: pending — Run typecheck + lint quality gates
   - 8: pending — Code-reviewer subagent
   - 9: pending — Commit, update prd.json and progress.txt
   
   The migration is fully applied and tested. Three follow-up migrations were needed due to errors with search_path pinning + function qualification. Final DB state has working triggers with hardened search_path.

9. Optional Next Step:
   Per the Ralph loop step 6, the next task is to run quality gates: `npx tsc --noEmit` and `npx expo lint`. After they pass, spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent (mandatory per Ralph rules) with the four migration files and src/types/supabase.ts as the diff scope, the US-007 acceptance criteria, and pointers to docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 and §15. Then commit and update prd.json + progress.txt.
   
   Direct quote from the most recent assistant message before the summary request: "All checks pass. Verifying advisors are now clean." → "Zero ERROR/WARN advisors; only the expected INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (deferred to US-009). Regenerating types now." The next concrete action would be to run `npx tsc --noEmit` followed by `npx expo lint` from the repo root, since types regeneration is now complete.

file.content:
-- US-007 follow-up #2: requalify trigger function bodies for empty search_path.
--
-- The previous migration set `search_path = ''` on both trigger functions to
-- clear the `function_search_path_mutable` advisor. With an empty search_path,
-- unqualified user-defined types (e.g. `platform`, `scrape_mode`) no longer
-- resolve. We rewrite both function bodies with `public.<type>` casts on enum
-- literals and `pg_catalog.<fn>` on built-ins. Restating `set search_path = ''`
-- because CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION does not preserve prior SET clauses.
--
-- NOTE: this version qualifies `greatest()` and `now()` as `pg_catalog.*`,
-- which is INCORRECT — `GREATEST` is a SQL parser built-in (not a callable
-- function) and cannot be schema-qualified. The runtime error surfaces only
-- when the trigger fires on a fresh tiktok_profile row. Fixed in
-- us_007_fix_function_refs (the next migration).

create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
returns trigger
language plpgsql
set search_path = ''
as $$
declare p public.platform;
begin
  select sl.platform into p from public.social_links sl where sl.id = new.social_link_id;
  if p is null then
    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
  end if;
  if (p = 'tiktok'::public.platform
        and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode)
  or (p = 'instagram'::public.platform
        and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details'::public.scrape_mode, 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode)) then
    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger
language plpgsql
security definer
set search_path = ''
as $$
declare
  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
  lock_key bigint;
begin
  if new.status <> 'fresh'::public.metric_status then
    new.is_latest := false;
    return new;
  end if;

  lock_key := pg_catalog.hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
  perform pg_catalog.pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);

  update public.metric_snapshots
     set is_latest = false
   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
     and is_latest;

  new.is_latest := true;

  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
           metrics_fetched_at       = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
           metrics_fetched_at       = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts'::public.scrape_mode then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           metrics_fetched_at          = pg_catalog.greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at                  = pg_catalog.now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;
  end if;

  return new;
end $$;

-- US-007 follow-up: pin search_path on the two new trigger functions.
--
-- get_advisors raised `function_search_path_mutable` (WARN) on
-- app_private.denorm_metrics and app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence.
-- denorm_metrics is SECURITY DEFINER, so a caller-controlled search_path could
-- shadow public.creator_profiles or public.metric_snapshots and divert writes.
-- Both functions already qualify every reference, so an empty search_path is
-- safe and clears the advisor.

alter function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
  set search_path = '';

alter function app_private.denorm_metrics()
  set search_path = '';

-- US-007: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger.
--
-- Per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) and the trigger
-- definitions in §15 (lines 1205-1291).
--
-- Three pieces:
--   1. public.metric_snapshots — historized rows, one per Apify run.
--   2. app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence — BEFORE INSERT/UPDATE
--      trigger ensuring scrape_mode matches the linked social_link.platform.
--   3. app_private.denorm_metrics — BEFORE INSERT trigger that, for fresh
--      rows, demotes the previous latest under a (social_link, scrape_mode)
--      advisory lock and mirrors the owned columns into creator_profiles.
--
-- Spec gap (story text vs. spec DDL): the story acceptance criterion says the
-- advisory lock is keyed on (user_id, scrape_mode); the spec keys it on
-- (social_link_id, scrape_mode). The two are functionally equivalent because
-- social_links has a partial unique index on (user_id, platform). Following
-- the spec verbatim.

-- =========================================================
-- 1. metric_snapshots table
-- =========================================================
create table public.metric_snapshots (
  id                uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid(),
  social_link_id    uuid not null references public.social_links(id) on delete cascade,
  scrape_mode       scrape_mode not null,
  apify_run_id      text,
  status            metric_status not null,
  follower_count    integer,
  following_count   integer,
  total_likes       bigint,
  video_count       integer,
  avg_views_last_10 integer,
  is_verified       boolean,
  raw_payload       jsonb,
  fetched_at        timestamptz not null default now(),
  is_latest         boolean not null default false,
  error_message     text
);

create index on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, fetched_at desc);

-- Only one "latest" snapshot per (social_link, scrape_mode); IG has at most two.
create unique index metric_snapshots_latest
  on public.metric_snapshots (social_link_id, scrape_mode) where is_latest;

-- Idempotency for webhook redeliveries — same Apify run never inserts twice.
create unique index metric_snapshots_run_uniq
  on public.metric_snapshots (apify_run_id) where apify_run_id is not null;

-- Janitor cron `fail-stuck-refreshing` scans here. Partial keeps it sub-ms.
create index metric_snapshots_stuck_idx
  on public.metric_snapshots (fetched_at) where status = 'refreshing';

-- =========================================================
-- 2. Coherence trigger: scrape_mode ↔ social_link.platform
-- =========================================================
create or replace function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence()
returns trigger language plpgsql as $$
declare p platform;
begin
  select platform into p from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;
  if p is null then
    raise exception 'social_link % not found', new.social_link_id;
  end if;
  if (p = 'tiktok'    and new.scrape_mode <> 'tiktok_profile')
  or (p = 'instagram' and new.scrape_mode not in ('ig_details', 'ig_posts')) then
    raise exception 'scrape_mode % incoherent with platform %', new.scrape_mode, p;
  end if;
  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_metric_snapshots_coherence
  before insert or update of scrape_mode, social_link_id on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.check_metric_snapshot_coherence();

-- =========================================================
-- 3. Race-free latest-row promotion + denormalization trigger
-- =========================================================
-- BEFORE INSERT so we can mutate new.is_latest; advisory lock serializes
-- concurrent webhook deliveries / manual refreshes for the same
-- (social_link, scrape_mode) pair.
create or replace function app_private.denorm_metrics()
returns trigger language plpgsql security definer as $$
declare
  sl       public.social_links%rowtype;
  lock_key bigint;
begin
  if new.status <> 'fresh' then
    -- 'refreshing' and 'failed' rows never become latest, never denormalize.
    new.is_latest := false;
    return new;
  end if;

  lock_key := hashtextextended(new.social_link_id::text || ':' || new.scrape_mode::text, 0);
  perform pg_advisory_xact_lock(lock_key);

  update public.metric_snapshots
     set is_latest = false
   where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
     and scrape_mode    = new.scrape_mode
     and is_latest;

  new.is_latest := true;

  select * into sl from public.social_links where id = new.social_link_id;

  if new.scrape_mode = 'tiktok_profile' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set tiktok_follower_count    = new.follower_count,
           tiktok_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           tiktok_total_likes       = new.total_likes,
           tiktok_video_count       = new.video_count,
           tiktok_is_verified       = new.is_verified,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_details' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_follower_count = new.follower_count,
           instagram_media_count    = new.video_count,
           metrics_fetched_at       = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at               = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;

  elsif new.scrape_mode = 'ig_posts' then
    update public.creator_profiles
       set instagram_avg_views_last_10 = new.avg_views_last_10,
           metrics_fetched_at          = greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at),
           updated_at                  = now()
     where user_id = sl.user_id;
  end if;

  return new;
end $$;

create trigger trg_denorm_metrics
  before insert on public.metric_snapshots
  for each row execute function app_private.denorm_metrics();

<EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>
You have superpowers.

**Below is the full content of your 'superpowers:using-superpowers' skill - your introduction to using skills. For all other skills, use the 'Skill' tool:**

---
name: using-superpowers
description: Use when starting any conversation - establishes how to find and use skills, requiring Skill tool invocation before ANY response including clarifying questions
---

<SUBAGENT-STOP>
If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific task, skip this skill.
</SUBAGENT-STOP>

<EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>
If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply to what you are doing, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill.

IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.
</EXTREMELY-IMPORTANT>

## Instruction Priority

Superpowers skills override default system prompt behavior, but **user instructions always take precedence**:

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, AGENTS.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Superpowers skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, or AGENTS.md says "don't use TDD" and a skill says "always use TDD," follow the user's instructions. The user is in control.

## How to Access Skills

**In Claude Code:** Use the `Skill` tool. When you invoke a skill, its content is loaded and presented to you—follow it directly. Never use the Read tool on skill files.

**In Copilot CLI:** Use the `skill` tool. Skills are auto-discovered from installed plugins. The `skill` tool works the same as Claude Code's `Skill` tool.

**In Gemini CLI:** Skills activate via the `activate_skill` tool. Gemini loads skill metadata at session start and activates the full content on demand.

**In other environments:** Check your platform's documentation for how skills are loaded.

## Platform Adaptation

Skills use Claude Code tool names. Non-CC platforms: see `references/copilot-tools.md` (Copilot CLI), `references/codex-tools.md` (Codex) for tool equivalents. Gemini CLI users get the tool mapping loaded automatically via GEMINI.md.

# Using Skills

## The Rule

**Invoke relevant or requested skills BEFORE any response or action.** Even a 1% chance a skill might apply means that you should invoke the skill to check. If an invoked skill turns out to be wrong for the situation, you don't need to use it.

```dot
digraph skill_flow {
    "User message received" [shape=doublecircle];
    "About to EnterPlanMode?" [shape=doublecircle];
    "Already brainstormed?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" [shape=box];
    "Might any skill apply?" [shape=diamond];
    "Invoke Skill tool" [shape=box];
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" [shape=box];
    "Has checklist?" [shape=diamond];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [shape=box];
    "Follow skill exactly" [shape=box];
    "Respond (including clarifications)" [shape=doublecircle];

    "About to EnterPlanMode?" -> "Already brainstormed?";
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Invoke brainstorming skill" [label="no"];
    "Already brainstormed?" -> "Might any skill apply?" [label="yes"];
    "Invoke brainstorming skill" -> "Might any skill apply?";

    "User message received" -> "Might any skill apply?";
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Invoke Skill tool" [label="yes, even 1%"];
    "Might any skill apply?" -> "Respond (including clarifications)" [label="definitely not"];
    "Invoke Skill tool" -> "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'";
    "Announce: 'Using [skill] to [purpose]'" -> "Has checklist?";
    "Has checklist?" -> "Create TodoWrite todo per item" [label="yes"];
    "Has checklist?" -> "Follow skill exactly" [label="no"];
    "Create TodoWrite todo per item" -> "Follow skill exactly";
}
```

## Red Flags

These thoughts mean STOP—you're rationalizing:

| Thought | Reality |
|---------|---------|
| "This is just a simple question" | Questions are tasks. Check for skills. |
| "I need more context first" | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. |
| "Let me explore the codebase first" | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first. |
| "I can check git/files quickly" | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills. |
| "Let me gather information first" | Skills tell you HOW to gather information. |
| "This doesn't need a formal skill" | If a skill exists, use it. |
| "I remember this skill" | Skills evolve. Read current version. |
| "This doesn't count as a task" | Action = task. Check for skills. |
| "The skill is overkill" | Simple things become complex. Use it. |
| "I'll just do this one thing first" | Check BEFORE doing anything. |
| "This feels productive" | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this. |
| "I know what that means" | Knowing the concept ≠ using the skill. Invoke it. |

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills could apply, use this order:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorming, debugging) - these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Implementation skills second** (frontend-design, mcp-builder) - these guide execution

"Let's build X" → brainstorming first, then implementation skills.
"Fix this bug" → debugging first, then domain-specific skills.

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, debugging): Follow exactly. Don't adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (patterns): Adapt principles to context.

The skill itself tells you which.

## User Instructions

Instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" doesn't mean skip workflows.


</EXTREMELY_IMPORTANT>

input.prompt:
You are reviewing the implementation of **US-007 — Migration: metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger** for the Marketify project (Expo/React Native + Supabase). This is a Supabase migration story; no app code is in scope.

## Files to review (all under `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify`)

1. `supabase/migrations/20260417120000_us_007_metric_snapshots.sql` — initial migration: table, indexes, two trigger functions, two triggers
2. `supabase/migrations/20260417120100_us_007_pin_function_search_path.sql` — follow-up #1: ALTER FUNCTION ... SET search_path = ''
3. `supabase/migrations/20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql` — follow-up #2 (partially-broken — see note below)
4. `supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql` — follow-up #3: final correct function bodies
5. `src/types/supabase.ts` — regenerated types (only the metric_snapshots additions are in scope; the rest is auto-generated)

**Important about migration #3 (`20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql`):** it was applied to the DB and contains a real bug (`pg_catalog.greatest()` and `pg_catalog.now()` — neither call resolves). Per a hard codebase rule, applied migration files MUST NOT be edited after the fact (drift between file and `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`). The file therefore retains the bug verbatim and the next migration `20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql` is the actual fix. The header comments in both files document this. Treat this pattern as correct — flag it only if you think the documentation is unclear.

## Authoritative spec

- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) — table DDL
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §15 (lines 1205-1291) — trigger function bodies

## US-007 acceptance criteria (verbatim from `scripts/ralph/prd.json`)

- `metric_snapshots` rows are inserted; `is_latest=true` exactly once per (social_link_id, scrape_mode) and previous row demoted under an advisory lock keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)`
- Denorm columns on `creator_profiles` reflect the most recent fresh row for the matching scrape_mode
- `metrics_fetched_at` updated to the latest `fetched_at`
- Coherence trigger rejects mismatched `(scrape_mode, platform)` pairs

**Spec gap to be aware of:** the story text says the advisory lock is keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)` but the spec DDL keys it on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`. They are functionally equivalent (`social_links` has a partial unique index on `(user_id, platform)` so given a fresh status row, there is at most one `social_link` per `(user_id, scrape_mode)` family). The implementation follows the spec verbatim — `social_link_id`. Treat this as a deliberate, documented decision; flag only if you disagree with the equivalence argument.

## End-to-end test results (already executed against the live DB)

All passed:
- 2 fresh tiktok_profile snapshots → newest `is_latest=true`, older demoted; denorm columns reflect newest values
- Coherence trigger rejects `ig_details` on a tiktok platform link
- `refreshing` status row neither claims `is_latest` nor clobbers denorm
- IG split: both `ig_details` and `ig_posts` rows on the same link both stay `is_latest=true` (count=2) without overwriting each other's columns
- Apify run_id idempotency: duplicate insert on the same `apify_run_id` triggers `unique_violation`
- Final `get_advisors`: zero ERROR/WARN; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (deferred to US-009 which adds the RLS policies)

## What I want from you

Focus on issues that would actually break or destabilize this story or future ones. Specifically check:

1. **Trigger semantics correctness** — Does `denorm_metrics` correctly handle every status × scrape_mode combination, including edge cases like `null` follower_count fields, the BEFORE INSERT mutation of `new.is_latest`, and the lock acquisition ordering vs. the demote UPDATE?
2. **SECURITY DEFINER hardening** — `denorm_metrics` runs as the function owner. Is the empty `search_path` + `public.<type>` qualification sufficient, or is there a residual write-redirection vector I missed? (The function inserts/updates only `public.metric_snapshots` and `public.creator_profiles`.)
3. **Index design** — Are the 4 indexes (1 base on `(social_link_id, fetched_at desc)`, 3 partial: `is_latest`, `apify_run_id`, `status='refreshing'`) correct for the workloads described in the spec? Anything missing?
4. **Data integrity** — Any path by which the unique constraint `metric_snapshots_latest` could be violated under concurrent inserts? The advisory lock is per `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`; is that scope right?
5. **Migration hygiene** — The 4-migration sequence is defensible (each fixed a real advisor/runtime issue and the sequence is documented in headers), but flag if you think any of the header comments are misleading or if a future engineer would be confused.
6. **Type drift** — Do the regenerated types in `src/types/supabase.ts` correctly reflect the new table? (Check Row/Insert/Update types for `metric_snapshots`.)

Format your response as a punch list of issues by severity:
- **Blocking** (would fail the story or destabilize prod)
- **Should fix before commit** (correctness or hygiene worth addressing now)
- **Nits** (style, optional improvements)
- **Approved as-is** (anything you reviewed and explicitly believe is fine)

Keep it under 600 words. If you find no blocking issues, say so explicitly so I can commit.

content.text:
I have everything I need. Here is the review.

---

## Review: US-007 — metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger

Reviewing 4 migration files and the regenerated TypeScript types against the spec in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 / §15.

---

### Blocking

None. No blocking issues found.

---

### Should fix before commit

**1. `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` silently clobbers denorm with stale data on out-of-order delivery (confidence: 82)**

`greatest()` returns the newer timestamp, which correctly guards `metrics_fetched_at` itself. But the surrounding column assignments (`tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, etc.) are set unconditionally — if Apify delivers a webhook for an older run after a newer one has already landed, the trigger will overwrite fresh values with stale ones even though `metrics_fetched_at` correctly refuses to move backward. The `greatest()` call gives a false sense of out-of-order protection.

The fix is to add a guard before each `UPDATE`:

```sql
-- skip if a newer fresh row already exists for this (social_link, scrape_mode)
if new.fetched_at >= (select max(fetched_at) from public.metric_snapshots
                       where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
                         and scrape_mode = new.scrape_mode
                         and status = 'fresh') then
  -- run the UPDATE
end if;
```

Or alternatively, add `AND new.fetched_at >= metrics_fetched_at` to each `UPDATE ... WHERE` clause. The apify_run_id unique index stops true duplicates, but a re-run with a fresh run_id and older data is a real scenario.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql`, lines 62-85 (and the equivalent in the spec at `docs/tech-architecture.md` lines 1259-1284 — the spec has the same gap).

---

**2. `hashtextextended` collision risk: advisory lock scope is one bigint for all `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)` pairs (confidence: 80)**

`hashtextextended` maps an arbitrary string to a signed 64-bit integer. Two different `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)` strings can collide to the same lock key, causing unrelated social links to serialize against each other. This is not a correctness issue (it can only cause unnecessary blocking, not data corruption), but under load with many creators it degrades throughput silently and is hard to diagnose. The standard pattern for Postgres advisory locks over two distinct values is `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))` (two-argument form), which uses a 32-bit × 32-bit key space and has no collision risk for this use case.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql`, line 48.

---

### Nits

**3. `now()` is stable, not volatile — minor semantic note**

The comment in migration #3 says `now()` resolves bare because `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched. That's correct, but `now()` in a PL/pgSQL function body returns the transaction start time, not the wall clock. This is almost certainly intentional (and correct for audit timestamps), but worth a one-line comment since a future reader may wonder why `clock_timestamp()` wasn't used.

**4. Migration #2 (`20260417120100`) header says "both functions already qualify every reference"**

This is inaccurate at the time that migration was written — the functions in migration #1 use bare `platform`, `scrape_mode`, and `metric_status` without schema qualification. The follow-on migrations fix this, but the header comment in #2 is misleading about the state of the functions it is altering. Low stakes since the sequence is documented, but a future bisect could be confusing.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120100_us_007_pin_function_search_path.sql`, line 6.

---

### Approved as-is

- **Trigger semantics (BEFORE INSERT mutation of `new.is_latest`):** Correct. Non-fresh rows have `is_latest` forced to `false` before the row lands; fresh rows have the demote UPDATE run first, then `new.is_latest := true` set, so the unique partial index on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode) WHERE is_latest` is never in a state where two rows are both `true` simultaneously within the same transaction.
- **SECURITY DEFINER hardening:** `set search_path = ''` + explicit `public.` qualification on all table references in migration #3 is sufficient. There is no residual write-redirection vector. `pg_catalog` functions (`hashtextextended`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock`) are correctly retained as schema-qualified.
- **Index design:** All four indexes match their stated workloads. The partial `is_latest` unique index correctly allows two `is_latest=true` rows per IG social link (different `scrape_mode`). The `apify_run_id` partial unique index correctly excludes nulls. The `status='refreshing'` partial index is correctly scoped for the janitor cron.
- **4-migration sequence hygiene:** The sequence is defensible and the headers in migrations #3 and #4 accurately document the applied-but-broken state of #3 and the rationale for #4. Future engineers will understand the chain.
- **TypeScript types:** The `Row`, `Insert`, and `Update` shapes in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts` (lines 366-427) correctly reflect the DDL. 3. `supabase/migrations/20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql` — follow-up #2 (partially-broken — see note below)
4. `supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql` — follow-up #3: final correct function bodies
5. `src/types/supabase.ts` — regenerated types (only the metric_snapshots additions are in scope; the rest is auto-generated)

**Important about migration #3 (`20260417120200_us_007_qualify_function_refs.sql`):** it was applied to the DB and contains a real bug (`pg_catalog.greatest()` and `pg_catalog.now()` — neither call resolves). Per a hard codebase rule, applied migration files MUST NOT be edited after the fact (drift between file and `supabase_migrations.schema_migrations`). The file therefore retains the bug verbatim and the next migration `20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql` is the actual fix. The header comments in both files document this. Treat this pattern as correct — flag it only if you think the documentation is unclear.

## Authoritative spec

- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 block 6 (lines 564-597) — table DDL
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §15 (lines 1205-1291) — trigger function bodies

## US-007 acceptance criteria (verbatim from `scripts/ralph/prd.json`)

- `metric_snapshots` rows are inserted; `is_latest=true` exactly once per (social_link_id, scrape_mode) and previous row demoted under an advisory lock keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)`
- Denorm columns on `creator_profiles` reflect the most recent fresh row for the matching scrape_mode
- `metrics_fetched_at` updated to the latest `fetched_at`
- Coherence trigger rejects mismatched `(scrape_mode, platform)` pairs

**Spec gap to be aware of:** the story text says the advisory lock is keyed on `(user_id, scrape_mode)` but the spec DDL keys it on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)`. They are functionally equivalent (`social_links` has a partial unique index on `(user_id, platform)` so given a fresh status row, there is at most one `social_link` per `(user_id, scrape_mode)` family). The implementation follows the spec verbatim — `social_link_id`. Treat this as a deliberate, documented decision; flag only if you disagree with the equivalence argument.

## End-to-end test results (already executed against the live DB)

All passed:
- 2 fresh tiktok_profile snapshots → newest `is_latest=true`, older demoted; denorm columns reflect newest values
- Coherence trigger rejects `ig_details` on a tiktok platform link
- `refreshing` status row neither claims `is_latest` nor clobbers denorm
- IG split: both `ig_details` and `ig_posts` rows on the same link both stay `is_latest=true` (count=2) without overwriting each other's columns
- Apify run_id idempotency: duplicate insert on the same `apify_run_id` triggers `unique_violation`
- Final `get_advisors`: zero ERROR/WARN; only INFO `rls_enabled_no_policy` (deferred to US-009 which adds the RLS policies)

## What I want from you

Focus on issues that would actually break or destabilize this story or future ones. Specifically check:

1. **Trigger semantics correctness** — Does `denorm_metrics` correctly handle every status × scrape_mode combination, including edge cases like `null` follower_count fields, the BEFORE INSERT mutation of `new.is_latest`, and the lock acquisition ordering vs. the demote UPDATE?

5. **Migration hygiene** — The 4-migration sequence is defensible (each fixed a real advisor/runtime issue and the sequence is documented in headers), but flag if you think any of the header comments are misleading or if a future engineer would be confused.
6. **Type drift** — Do the regenerated types in `src/types/supabase.ts` correctly reflect the new table? (Check Row/Insert/Update types for `metric_snapshots`.)

Format your response as a punch list of issues by severity:

content.text:
I have everything I need. Here is the review.

---

## Review: US-007 — metric_snapshots + denormalization trigger

Reviewing 4 migration files and the regenerated TypeScript types against the spec in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 / §15.

---

### Blocking

None. No blocking issues found.

---

### Should fix before commit

**1. `greatest(metrics_fetched_at, new.fetched_at)` silently clobbers denorm with stale data on out-of-order delivery (confidence: 82)**

`greatest()` returns the newer timestamp, which correctly guards `metrics_fetched_at` itself. But the surrounding column assignments (`tiktok_follower_count`, `instagram_follower_count`, etc.) are set unconditionally — if Apify delivers a webhook for an older run after a newer one has already landed, the trigger will overwrite fresh values with stale ones even though `metrics_fetched_at` correctly refuses to move backward. The `greatest()` call gives a false sense of out-of-order protection.

The fix is to add a guard before each `UPDATE`:

```sql
-- skip if a newer fresh row already exists for this (social_link, scrape_mode)
if new.fetched_at >= (select max(fetched_at) from public.metric_snapshots
                       where social_link_id = new.social_link_id
                         and scrape_mode = new.scrape_mode
                         and status = 'fresh') then
  -- run the UPDATE
end if;
```

Or alternatively, add `AND new.fetched_at >= metrics_fetched_at` to each `UPDATE ... WHERE` clause. The apify_run_id unique index stops true duplicates, but a re-run with a fresh run_id and older data is a real scenario.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql`, lines 62-85 (and the equivalent in the spec at `docs/tech-architecture.md` lines 1259-1284 — the spec has the same gap).

---

**2. `hashtextextended` collision risk: advisory lock scope is one bigint for all `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)` pairs (confidence: 80)**

`hashtextextended` maps an arbitrary string to a signed 64-bit integer. Two different `(social_link_id, scrape_mode)` strings can collide to the same lock key, causing unrelated social links to serialize against each other. This is not a correctness issue (it can only cause unnecessary blocking, not data corruption), but under load with many creators it degrades throughput silently and is hard to diagnose. The standard pattern for Postgres advisory locks over two distinct values is `pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtext(social_link_id::text), hashtext(scrape_mode::text))` (two-argument form), which uses a 32-bit × 32-bit key space and has no collision risk for this use case.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120300_us_007_fix_function_refs.sql`, line 48.

---

### Nits

**3. `now()` is stable, not volatile — minor semantic note**

The comment in migration #3 says `now()` resolves bare because `pg_catalog` is always implicitly searched. That's correct, but `now()` in a PL/pgSQL function body returns the transaction start time, not the wall clock. This is almost certainly intentional (and correct for audit timestamps), but worth a one-line comment since a future reader may wonder why `clock_timestamp()` wasn't used.

**4. Migration #2 (`20260417120100`) header says "both functions already qualify every reference"**

This is inaccurate at the time that migration was written — the functions in migration #1 use bare `platform`, `scrape_mode`, and `metric_status` without schema qualification. The follow-on migrations fix this, but the header comment in #2 is misleading about the state of the functions it is altering. Low stakes since the sequence is documented, but a future bisect could be confusing.

File: `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/supabase/migrations/20260417120100_us_007_pin_function_search_path.sql`, line 6.

---

### Approved as-is

- **Trigger semantics (BEFORE INSERT mutation of `new.is_latest`):** Correct. Non-fresh rows have `is_latest` forced to `false` before the row lands; fresh rows have the demote UPDATE run first, then `new.is_latest := true` set, so the unique partial index on `(social_link_id, scrape_mode) WHERE is_latest` is never in a state where two rows are both `true` simultaneously within the same transaction.
- **SECURITY DEFINER hardening:** `set search_path = ''` + explicit `public.` qualification on all table references in migration #3 is sufficient. There is no residual write-redirection vector. `pg_catalog` functions (`hashtextextended`, `pg_advisory_xact_lock`) are correctly retained as schema-qualified.
- **Index design:** All four indexes match their stated workloads. The partial `is_latest` unique index correctly allows two `is_latest=true` rows per IG social link (different `scrape_mode`). The `apify_run_id` partial unique index correctly excludes nulls. The `status='refreshing'` partial index is correctly scoped for the janitor cron.
- **4-migration sequence hygiene:** The sequence is defensible and the headers in migrations #3 and #4 accurately document the applied-but-broken state of #3 and the rationale for #4. Future engineers will understand the chain.
- **TypeScript types:** The `Row`, `Insert`, and `Update` shapes in `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/src/types/supabase.ts` (lines 366-427) correctly reflect the DDL. All nullable columns are typed `| null`, required fields (`scrape_mode`, `social_link_id`, `status`) are non-optional in `Insert`, defaults (`id`, `fetched_at`, `is_latest`) are optional in `Insert`. The FK relationship to `social_links` is present. `total_likes` is correctly `number | null` (maps to `bigint`; Supabase JS client serializes bigint as number). No drift detected.

---

**Summary:** No blocking issues. Issue #1 (stale-write on out-of-order delivery) is the most important thing to resolve before this is relied upon by the Apify webhook handler, as it is a silent data correctness bug that the end-to-end tests would not have caught (the tests used sequentially inserted rows, not out-of-order ones). Issue #2 is a low-probability throughput concern worth a one-line fix.

# Marketify — Ralph Agent Instructions

You are an autonomous coding agent building **Marketify**, a mobile marketplace where creators claim bounties from listers (Expo + React Native + Supabase). Each invocation is a fresh Claude Code instance with no prior memory. Every persistent decision lives in git history, `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`, and `scripts/ralph/prd.json`.

---

## Authoritative specs (read these for any ambiguity)

| File | What it defines |
|---|---|
| `docs/product-plan.md` | Canonical product, 12-step build sequence, scope cuts |
| `docs/design.md` | Visual language, component inventory, screen catalog, motion |
| `docs/tech-architecture.md` | DB schema (§4.7), RLS, triggers, Apify integration (§3), edge-function spec, eligibility engine |

If a story is ambiguous, the specs win. Do **not** invent product behavior the docs don't sanction. If something is genuinely missing, append a `Spec gap:` note to `progress.txt` and pick the simplest interpretation that aligns with the docs.

---

## The loop you run, every iteration

1. **Orient.** Read `scripts/ralph/prd.json` and `scripts/ralph/progress.txt`. Pay special attention to the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt` — it consolidates everything previous iterations learned.
2. **Branch.** Make sure you're on the branch named in `prd.json.branchName`. If it doesn't exist, create it from `main`.
3. **Pick one story.** Lowest `priority` number where `passes: false`. Work on **exactly one** story per iteration.
4. **Research before coding.** If understanding the story requires reading >3 files, **delegate to the Explore subagent** (`subagent_type: "Explore"`) so your own context stays clean for implementation. Pass it the story plus pointers into the docs.
5. **Implement.** Make the minimal change set that satisfies every acceptance criterion. Match existing conventions. Don't refactor adjacent code.
6. **Quality gates** (run in this order, stop on first failure and fix before continuing):
   - `npx tsc --noEmit` — must pass with zero errors
   - `npx expo lint` — must pass (warnings allowed only if pre-existing in main)
   - Migration sanity: any new SQL must apply cleanly via Supabase MCP without manual fix-up
7. **Code review (mandatory before commit).** Spawn the `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent with the diff scope, the story acceptance criteria, and the relevant doc sections. Address every issue rated medium or higher. Re-run quality gates after fixes.
8. **Mobile verification (mandatory for any UI story).** Use `mobile-mcp` to:
   - Launch the app on the iOS simulator (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_launch_app`)
   - Navigate to the screen the story touches
   - Exercise the golden path
   - Take a screenshot (`mcp__mobile-mcp__mobile_take_screenshot`) and reference it in the progress note
   - If the simulator is unavailable, **do not silently skip** — set `passes: false`, write a `notes` entry on the story explaining the blocker, and move to the next story.
9. **Commit.** Stage everything you changed (no `git add -A` blanket — list paths). Commit message format:
   ```
   feat: [US-XXX] Story title
   ```
10. **Mark done.** In `prd.json`, set `passes: true` for the story you just finished. Append a dated entry to `progress.txt` (format below).
11. **Stop check.** If every story has `passes: true`, your final response must contain `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` and nothing else useful. Otherwise end normally — the next iteration picks up.

---

## MCP servers — use these instead of CLI wherever possible

### Supabase (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`) — required for ALL database work
- `apply_migration` — every schema change. Migrations are append-only and named after the story (e.g. `us_006_metric_snapshots_denorm_trigger`).
- `execute_sql` — read-only queries, RLS verification, seed data inspection.
- `list_tables`, `list_migrations`, `list_extensions` — orient before changing things.
- `generate_typescript_types` — regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` after every migration. Commit the regenerated file with the migration.
- `deploy_edge_function`, `get_edge_function`, `list_edge_functions` — for `apify-core`, `auth-*`, `metrics-refresh`, the webhook receiver, etc.
- `get_advisors` — run with `type: "security"` after any schema or RLS change. Address every error-level advisor before committing.
- `get_logs` — when an edge function or query misbehaves.

Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for migrations or queries. The MCP is the source of truth so iterations stay consistent.

### Mobile (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`) — required for any UI verification
- `mobile_list_available_devices` then `mobile_launch_app` to boot the app.
- `mobile_list_elements_on_screen` + `mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates` to drive flows.
- `mobile_take_screenshot` after each meaningful state — name screenshots `us-XXX-state.png` and store under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`.
- Use `mobile_type_keys` rather than tapping virtual keys when filling text inputs.

### Context7 (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`) — for any library docs
Use **before** writing non-trivial integration code with Expo, Reanimated, NativeWind, Supabase JS, React Navigation, etc. Your training data may be stale. `resolve-library-id` then `query-docs`.

### Apify
The API key is in `.env` as `APIFY_KEY`. Always read it at runtime via `process.env.APIFY_KEY` (or `Deno.env.get("APIFY_KEY")` inside edge functions). **Never** paste the key into source. Actor catalog and input schemas live in `docs/tech-architecture.md` §3.

---

## Subagent delegation — preserve your context

You will burn out your own context window if you try to do everything in-line. Delegate aggressively:

| When | Subagent | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Need to read >3 files to understand the codebase | `Explore` | Returns a focused summary instead of dumping raw file contents into your context |
| Before every commit | `feature-dev:code-reviewer` | Independent review against acceptance criteria + doc sections |
| Implementation will touch >5 files (e.g. wiring a new edge function end-to-end) | `feature-dev:code-architect` first, then implement yourself | Plan once, execute once |
| Need to research how something works before changing it | `feature-dev:code-explorer` | Maps the existing layers without polluting your context |
| Open-ended search across the repo | `general-purpose` | Iterates until it finds the answer |

When briefing a subagent, give it the story ID, acceptance criteria, and a pointer to the relevant `docs/*.md` section. Do **not** tell it "based on your findings, implement" — synthesize the result yourself.

---

## Project quality conventions (these become permanent — additions go in `progress.txt` Codebase Patterns)

- **TypeScript everywhere.** No `any` without a `// eslint-disable-next-line` and a comment explaining why. Generated Supabase types are imported from `src/types/supabase.ts`.
- **Migrations are append-only.** Never edit a previously applied migration. If you need to change something, write a new migration that alters the prior state.
- **Edge functions** live in `supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts`. Each one has a one-paragraph header comment naming the contract (request shape → response shape) and the auth requirement.
- **RLS is mandatory** on every table that holds user data. After applying any RLS migration, run `get_advisors({type: "security"})` and fix every finding before committing.
- **Component organization:** primitives in `src/components/primitives/`, screen-specific composites in `src/screens/<screen>/`, shared composites in `src/components/shared/`.
- **No emoji** anywhere in the product UI (per `docs/design.md` — Lucide icons only).
- **No payments code** in v1 — `price_cents` is display-only.
- **Dual-role accounts** are out of scope for v1. One role per account.

---

## Progress log format (`progress.txt`)

Append (never replace) using this format:

```
## YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM — US-XXX
- What was implemented (1–3 bullets, concrete)
- Files changed (bulleted list)
- Quality checks: tsc ✓ / lint ✓ / supabase advisors ✓ / mobile-mcp ✓
- Code-reviewer verdict: [approved | issues fixed in this commit]
- **Learnings for future iterations:**
  - Patterns discovered
  - Gotchas
  - Useful pointers
---
```

If a learning is **general and reusable across stories**, also promote it into the `## Codebase Patterns` section at the top of `progress.txt`. That section is what every future iteration leans on first — keep it tight, deduplicated, and signal-only.

---

## Stop condition

After updating `prd.json`, count remaining `passes: false` stories. If zero, your last line in this response must be exactly:

```
<promise>COMPLETE</promise>
```

Otherwise end normally and let the loop spawn the next iteration.

---

## Hard rules (do not violate)

- **One story per iteration.** Don't peek ahead.
- **Never commit broken code.** A failing typecheck means you don't commit, you don't mark `passes: true`, you write a `notes` entry, and the next iteration retries.
- **Never skip the code-reviewer subagent.** It is the second pair of eyes that keeps quality from drifting across iterations.
- **Never skip mobile verification on UI stories.** A screen "looking right in the JSX" is not the same as it actually rendering correctly.
- **Never read `.env` contents into the conversation.** Reference variable names only.
- **Never edit `docs/product-plan.md`, `docs/design.md`, or `docs/tech-architecture.md`** unless a story explicitly says to. They are specs, not work product.
- **Never invent stories** or add new ones to `prd.json` mid-loop. If you discover a missing piece, append it as a `Spec gap:` line in `progress.txt` so the human can decide.
